official_pick_rules.note: Lab Rating is setup quality. It is not win probability. Official picks require both strong win probability and strong setup quality.

summary: No official picks cleared the stricter rules. 1 value-watch setup and 5 watchlist games remain research-only. 1 game reached a Lab Rating of 7.5+/10 but did not clear every official gate.

lab_score_breakdown.note: Lab Rating grades setup quality. It is not win probability.

games.read: Seattle Mariners is a value watch, not an official pick. LyDia projects 66.1% against a 59.8% no-vig market number, a 6.4% model edge at -155. Lab Rating 8.4/10: model edge 27.9, pitcher 25, bullpen 11.58, market 15, base 5. Seattle Mariners's pen efficiency: Dominant (7.9/10). Bullpen fatigue moved this probability up 1.6 points from 64.6% (starting pitcher and team strength only) to 66.1%. It stayed a value watch because model win probability is 66.1%, below the 72.0% official-pick gate. Lineup check: Seattle Mariners is a touch below its season form (-0.045 OPS); Cincinnati Reds is swinging a hot bat (+0.094 OPS). Recent form leans against this side — noted, not modeled.

model_v3.note: shadow A/B — does not drive picks

pick_team.label: Tired
pick_team.workload_read: Elevated recent workload. Full-game angles deserve extra late-inning caution.
pick_team.score_reason: Tired (6.5/10): 8 recent relief innings, recency-weighted (pitched yesterday), 5 arms worked back-to-back days. Recent innings count more; rest days lower the score. Workload only — see efficiency for how well the pen has pitched.
pick_team.formula: 45 + (recency-weighted (BP IP - 3/game) x 4.2) + (recency-weighted back-to-back arms x 8). Each game decays by half every 2 days, so rest days lower the score.

context_only.note: Reliever counts are displayed for context only and do not add score points.

pick_team.efficiency_reason: Dominant (7.9/10): 1.13 ERA and 0.63 WHIP over 8 relief innings (1 ER, 2 H, 3 BB) in the last three days.
pick_team.efficiency_formula: 50 - clamp((ERA - 4.20) x 6, -25, 25) x confidence - clamp((WHIP - 1.30) x 15, -20, 20) x confidence, confidence = clamp(BP IP / 6, 0.35, 1)
